summary: The implementation covers all major requirements well: backend problem generation (REQ-1), score storage/retrieval (REQ-2), game screen with 2-minute timer (REQ-3), start screen (REQ-4), results screen with leaderboard (REQ-5), and energetic dark visual theme (REQ-6). There is one notable gap: the backend problem number ranges do not match the spec (1–50 for add/sub, 1–20 for harder ops), as the backend uses 1–100 for all operands uniformly. The getLeaderboard function also calls scoresArray.sort() without a comparator despite the Score.compare module being defined, which is an implementation quality issue. Overall, all requirements are represented in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Problem numbers are within a reasonable range for mental math (e.g., 1–20 for harder ops, 1–50 for addition/subtraction)
missing_requirements.reason: The backend uses natRange(1, 101) for all operands across all operations, generating numbers up to 100 for multiplication and division as well. The spec requires tighter ranges for harder operations (1–20) versus easier ones (1–50).

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity authentication integration (InternetIdentityProvider, useInternetIdentity hook, AuthClient setup)
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request explicitly lists user authentication or Internet Identity login as a non-goal, yet the frontend wraps the app in an InternetIdentityProvider and includes a full useInternetIdentity hook with AuthClient.

hallucinated_features.description: Accuracy percentage stat displayed during gameplay
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec does not mention showing accuracy percentage; only score and timer are specified as prominently visible during gameplay.

hallucinated_features.description: Motivational score message tiers on results screen
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec does not request tiered motivational messages on the results screen; it only requires showing total correct answers prominently.
